annotations.label: Lattice (informal)

anchor.snippet: It consists of a partially ordered set in which every pair of elements has a unique supremum

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Lattice` class extends `SemilatticeSup` and `SemilatticeInf`, giving every pair a supremum and infimum.

annotations.label: Power set lattice

anchor.snippet: An example is given by the power set of a set, partially ordered by inclusion

annotations.note: `Set Î±` has a `BooleanAlgebra` instance, hence a (complete) lattice instance under inclusion.

annotations.label: Naturals under divisibility

anchor.snippet: Another example is given by the natural numbers , partially ordered by divisibility

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.gcd`/`Nat.lcm` as a `GCDMonoid`, and `GCDMonoid` gives a lattice on associates, but there is no direct `Lattice â„•` instance using divisibility as the order.

annotations.label: Lattice as poset

anchor.snippet: is called a lattice if it is both a join- and a meet- semilattice

annotations.note: `class Lattice extends SemilatticeSup Î±, SemilatticeInf Î±` is exactly this definition.

annotations.label: Finite subsets have lub/glb

anchor.snippet: It follows by an induction argument that every non-empty finite subset of a lattice has a least upper bound and a greatest lower bound

annotations.note: `Finset.sup'`/`Finset.inf'` provide the supremum/infimum of a nonempty finite subset in any semilattice.

annotations.label: Partial lattice

anchor.snippet: The resulting structure on

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a partial lattice with partially defined sup/inf operations.

annotations.label: Lattice as algebraic structure

anchor.snippet: A lattice is an algebraic structure

annotations.note: `Lattice.mk'` constructs a `Lattice` from commutative, associative `âŠ”`, `âŠ“` satisfying the two absorption laws.

anchors.snippet: A lattice is an algebraic structure

annotations.label: Idempotent laws follow from absorption

anchor.snippet: The following two identities are also usually regarded as axioms, even though they follow from the two absorption laws taken together

annotations.note: Idempotence is derived inside `Lattice.mk'` from `sup_inf_self` and `inf_sup_self`, but there is no standalone named lemma.

anchors.snippet: The following two identities are also usually regarded as axioms, even though they follow from the two absorption laws taken together

annotations.label: Order lattice yields algebraic lattice

anchor.snippet: An order-theoretic lattice gives rise to the two binary operations

annotations.note: The `Lattice` class bundles `Max`/`Min` and proves commutativity, associativity, and absorption (e.g. `sup_assoc`, `sup_inf_self`).

annotations.label: Algebraic lattice yields order

anchor.snippet: The converse is also true

annotations.note: `Lattice.mk'` takes the algebraic axioms and constructs the induced partial order `a â‰¤ b â†” a âŠ” b = b`.

anchors.value: {\displaystyle a=a\wedge b{\text{ implies }}b=b\vee (b\wedge a)=(a\wedge b)\vee b=a\vee b}

annotations.label: Bounded lattice

anchor.snippet: A bounded lattice is a lattice that additionally has a greatest element

annotations.note: A bounded lattice is encoded by `[Lattice Î±] [BoundedOrder Î±]`, where `BoundedOrder` extends `OrderTop` and `OrderBot`.

anchors.snippet: A bounded lattice is a lattice that additionally has a greatest element

annotations.label: Bounded lattice (algebraic)

anchor.snippet: A bounded lattice may also be defined as an algebraic structure of the form

annotations.note: Mathlib axiomatizes bounded lattices via `[Lattice Î±] [BoundedOrder Î±]` rather than as a single algebraic signature with `âŠ¥`/`âŠ¤` constants.

anchors.snippet: A bounded lattice may also be defined as an algebraic structure of the form

annotations.label: Bounded lattice characterization

anchor.snippet: It can be shown that a partially ordered set is a bounded lattice if and only if every finite set of elements (including the empty set) has a join and a meet

annotations.note: No explicit Mathlib lemma states the equivalence between bounded-lattice structure and the existence of joins/meets of all finite sets including the empty set.

annotations.label: Embedding into bounded lattice

anchor.snippet: Every lattice can be embedded into a bounded lattice by adding a greatest and a least element

annotations.note: `WithTop`/`WithBot` adjoin a top/bottom giving lattice instances, but there is no single packaged theorem stating that every lattice embeds into a bounded one.

annotations.label: Power set lattice

anchor.snippet: the collection of all subsets of

annotations.note: `Set Î±` carries a Boolean algebra (hence lattice) structure under union and intersection.

annotations.label: Finite subsets lattice

anchor.snippet: the collection of all finite subsets of

annotations.note: `Finset Î±` has a `Lattice` instance with `âˆª` and `âˆ©` as join and meet.

annotations.label: Partition lattice

anchor.snippet: the collection of all partitions of

annotations.note: `Setoid Î±` (equivalent to partitions) has a `CompleteLattice` instance refining the partition lattice.

annotations.label: Positive integers lattice

anchor.snippet: The positive integers in their usual order form an unbounded lattice

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Lattice â„•` instance comes from the linear order; restriction to positive integers is immediate.

annotations.label: Cartesian square of naturals

anchor.snippet: The Cartesian square of the natural numbers

annotations.note: `Prod.instLattice` gives `Î± Ã— Î²` a lattice structure componentwise, applicable to `â„• Ã— â„•`.

annotations.label: Naturals under gcd/lcm

anchor.snippet: The natural numbers also form a lattice under the operations of taking the greatest common divisor and least common multiple

annotations.note: `GCDMonoid â„•` provides `gcd`/`lcm`; the comment in `Mathlib.Algebra.GCDMonoid.Basic` notes gcd/lcm form a lattice on associates but no direct `Lattice â„•`-by-divisibility instance is provided.

annotations.label: Complete lattices are bounded

anchor.snippet: Every complete lattice (also see below ) is a (rather specific) bounded lattice

annotations.note: `CompleteLattice` extends `Lattice` and provides `âŠ¤`/`âŠ¥` via `sSup`/`sInf` of the empty/full set, yielding a `BoundedOrder`.

annotations.label: Compact elements of arithmetic lattice

anchor.snippet: The set of compact elements of an arithmetic complete lattice is a lattice with a least element

annotations.note: `IsCompactElement` is defined and `isCompactElement_finsetSup` exists, but the lattice structure on the set of compact elements in arithmetic lattices is not packaged as a single declaration.

annotations.label: Discrete poset non-lattice

anchor.snippet: A discrete poset, meaning a poset such that

annotations.note: This is a counterexample (a discrete poset of >1 element is not a lattice); no specific Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Divisibility non-lattice

anchor.snippet: so ordered is not a lattice because the pair 2, 3 lacks a join

annotations.note: Counterexample about a finite divisibility poset; not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three-bound non-lattice

anchor.snippet: Every pair of elements has an upper bound and a lower bound, but the pair 2, 3 has three upper bounds

annotations.note: A specific counterexample poset; not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lattice homomorphism

anchor.snippet: a lattice homomorphism from L to M is a function

annotations.note: `LatticeHom` is defined as a map extending `SupHom` and `InfHom`, i.e. preserving `âŠ”` and `âŠ“`.

anchors.snippet: a lattice homomorphism from L to M is a function

annotations.label: Bounded-lattice homomorphism

anchor.snippet: a bounded-lattice homomorphism (usually called just "lattice homomorphism")

annotations.note: `BoundedLatticeHom` is the type of maps preserving `âŠ¤`, `âŠ¥`, `âŠ”`, and `âŠ“`.

anchors.snippet: a bounded-lattice homomorphism (usually called just "lattice homomorphism")

annotations.label: Homomorphisms are monotone

anchor.snippet: Any homomorphism of lattices is necessarily monotone with respect to the associated ordering relation

annotations.note: Mathlib derives `OrderHomClass` from `SupHomClass`, so any lattice homomorphism is automatically monotone.

annotations.label: Lattice isomorphism/endomorphism/automorphism

anchor.snippet: a lattice isomorphism is just a bijective lattice homomorphism

annotations.note: Lattice isomorphisms can be represented by `OrderIso` (which induces a `LatticeHomClass` via `OrderIsoClass.toLatticeHomClass`), but there is no dedicated `LatticeIso`/endomorphism/automorphism type.

annotations.label: 0,1-separating homomorphism

anchor.snippet: is called 0 , 1 - separating if and only if

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define a notion of 0,1-separating lattice homomorphism.

annotations.label: Sublattice

anchor.snippet: A sublattice of a lattice

annotations.note: `structure Sublattice` is defined as a subset closed under `âŠ”` and `âŠ“`.

annotations.label: Convex sublattice

anchor.snippet: is a convex sublattice of

annotations.note: No `Sublattice.IsConvex`/convex sublattice predicate; `Set.OrdConnected` exists but is on sets, not specifically sublattices.

annotations.label: Complete lattice

anchor.snippet: A poset is called a complete lattice if all its subsets have both a join and a meet

annotations.note: `class CompleteLattice` extends `Lattice` together with `CompleteSemilatticeSup`/`CompleteSemilatticeInf`.

annotations.label: Complete semilattice is complete lattice

anchor.snippet: Every poset that is a complete semilattice is also a complete lattice

annotations.note: Mathlib promotes a `CompleteSemilatticeSup` to a `CompleteLattice` via `completeLatticeOfCompleteSemilatticeSup`.

annotations.label: Conditionally complete lattice

anchor.snippet: A conditionally complete lattice is a lattice in which every nonempty subset that has an upper bound has a join

annotations.note: `class ConditionallyCompleteLattice` extends `Lattice` with `SupSet`/`InfSet` operations valid on nonempty bounded sets.

annotations.label: Distributive lattice

anchor.snippet: A lattice that satisfies the first or, equivalently (as it turns out), the second axiom, is called a distributive lattice

annotations.note: `class DistribLattice` extends `Lattice` with the `le_sup_inf` axiom, equivalent to either distributive identity.

annotations.label: M3-N5 characterization of distributivity

anchor.snippet: A lattice is distributive if and only if it does not have a sublattice isomorphic to M 3 or N 5

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the M3/N5 forbidden-sublattice characterization of distributive lattices.

annotations.label: Birkhoff representation

anchor.snippet: Each distributive lattice is isomorphic to a lattice of sets

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Order.Birkhoff` proves Birkhoff representation for finite distributive lattices (as a lattice of sets).

annotations.label: Modular lattice

anchor.snippet: is modular if, for all elements

annotations.note: `class IsModularLattice` asserts `(x âŠ” y) âŠ“ z â‰¤ x âŠ” y âŠ“ z` whenever `x â‰¤ z`.

annotations.label: Modular inequality holds generally

anchor.snippet: In fact, the inequality

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `sup_inf_le : a âŠ” b âŠ“ c â‰¤ (a âŠ” b) âŠ“ (a âŠ” c)` in any lattice; the precise modular-inequality form `x â‰¤ z â†’ x âŠ” (y âŠ“ z) â‰¤ (x âŠ” y) âŠ“ z` for any lattice has no standalone name.

annotations.label: N5 characterization of modularity

anchor.snippet: A lattice is modular if and only if it does not have a sublattice isomorphic to N 5

annotations.note: The N5 forbidden-sublattice characterization of modular lattices is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Examples of modular lattices

anchor.snippet: examples of modular lattices are the lattice of submodules of a module

annotations.note: Mathlib has `instance : IsModularLattice (Submodule R M)` (and a similar instance for `LieSubmodule`).

annotations.label: Semimodular characterization

anchor.snippet: A finite lattice is modular if and only if it is both upper and lower semimodular

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsUpperModularLattice`/`IsLowerModularLattice` but does not prove the finite-lattice modular â†” upper+lower semimodular equivalence.

annotations.label: Lower semimodular lattice

anchor.snippet: A lattice is called lower semimodular if its dual is semimodular

annotations.note: `class IsLowerModularLattice` captures lower semimodularity via the covering relation.

annotations.label: Continuous and algebraic lattice

anchor.snippet: A continuous lattice is a complete lattice that is continuous as a poset

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have continuous-lattice or algebraic-lattice classes (only `IsCompactlyGenerated`, a related but distinct notion).

annotations.label: Complement

anchor.snippet: are complements of each other if and only if

annotations.note: `structure IsCompl x y` records that `x âŠ“ y = âŠ¥` and `x âŠ” y = âŠ¤`.

anchors.snippet: are complements of each other if and only if

annotations.label: Complemented lattice

anchor.snippet: A bounded lattice for which every element has a complement is called a complemented lattice

annotations.note: `class ComplementedLattice` asserts every element has some `IsCompl` partner.

annotations.label: Boolean algebra characterization

anchor.snippet: A complemented lattice that is also distributive is a Boolean algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `BooleanAlgebra` as a distributive bounded lattice with complement operation, but does not state "complemented distributive lattice â‡’ Boolean algebra" as a constructive promotion.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of complement in distributive lattice

anchor.snippet: For a distributive lattice, the complement of

annotations.note: An explicit comment in `Mathlib.Order.BooleanAlgebra.Basic` flags this as TODO; no general uniqueness-of-complement lemma exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pseudo-complement

anchor.snippet: has, on the other hand, a pseudo-complement

annotations.note: Mathlib has `HeytingAlgebra` (with `compl` as relative-bot-complement) which captures pseudo-complements in that setting, but no dedicated `PseudoComplement` definition for general meet-semilattices.

annotations.label: Chain and length

anchor.snippet: A chain from

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Set.chainHeight` (maximal length of a chain) and `RelSeries.length`; the article's specific definition of chain length between two elements is not packaged separately.

annotations.label: Jordanâ€“Dedekind chain condition

anchor.snippet: all maximal chains from

annotations.note: The Jordanâ€“Dedekind chain condition is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graded lattice

anchor.snippet: is called graded , sometimes ranked

annotations.note: `class GradeOrder` provides a grade function preserving the covering relation, matching the graded/ranked-lattice notion.

annotations.label: Covers relation

anchor.snippet: is said to cover another element

annotations.note: `def CovBy a b` says `a < b` with nothing strictly between, exactly the covering relation.

annotations.label: Free semilattice

anchor.snippet: The free semilattice is defined to consist of all of the finite subsets of

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `FreeSemilattice`/`FreeLattice` construction.

annotations.label: Flat lattice

anchor.snippet: may also be used to define a flat lattice

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define a flat lattice (the construction `WithBot (WithTop Î±)` of a discrete set exists implicitly but is not named).

annotations.label: Join irreducible

anchor.snippet: Join irreducible if

annotations.note: `def SupIrred a` captures sup-irreducibility: `a` is not minimal and `a = b âŠ” c â†’ a = b âˆ¨ a = c`.

annotations.label: Join prime

anchor.snippet: Join prime if

annotations.note: `def SupPrime a` captures sup-primality: `a` is not minimal and `a â‰¤ b âŠ” c â†’ a â‰¤ b âˆ¨ a â‰¤ c`.

annotations.label: Join-prime implies join-irreducible

anchor.snippet: Every join-prime element is also join irreducible

annotations.note: `SupPrime.supIrred` proves sup-primality implies sup-irreducibility.

annotations.label: Atom

anchor.snippet: is an atom if

annotations.note: `def IsAtom a` defines an atom as a minimal nonzero element.

annotations.label: Atomic and atomistic lattices

anchor.snippet: Atomic if for every nonzero element

annotations.note: `class IsAtomic` and `class IsAtomistic` formalize atomicity and atomisticity for `OrderBot`s.
